events.label: shell (exit 127)
events.text:
/bin/sh -lc "pwd && ls -la && cat > /app/answer.json <<'EOF'
{
  \"decision\": \"fail\",
  \"risk\": \"critical\",
  \"work_stage\": \"in-progress\",
  \"component_conditions\": [\"installed-defective\", \"non-compliant\"],
  \"percent_complete\": 50,
  \"value\": 0,
  \"sound_source\": \"\",
  \"confidence\": 0.93,
  \"event_time\": 0.0,
  \"rate\": 0.0,
  \"order\": [
    \"stop-hot-work\",
    \"make-area-safe\",
    \"evaluate-sewer-drain-hazard\",
    \"isolate-cover-or-seal-drain\",
    \"gas-test-and-verify-safe-atmosphere\",
    \"reissue-hot-work-permit\",
    \"resume-only-with-fire-watch-and-fire-protection\"
  ],
  \"workable\": [],
  \"findings\": [
    \"Hot work is being performed over a sewer or drain before the drain hazard has been controlled.\",
    \"Sewer or drain openings can carry flammable vapors and can admit sparks, slag, or heat into a concealed combustible or explosive atmosphere.\",
    \"The required hot work sequence was violated because fire/explosion prevention controls and permit verification must be complete before ignition sources are introduced.\"
  ],
  \"actions\": [
    \"Stop hot work immediately and remove or secure ignition sources.\",
    \"Post a fire watch and maintain fire extinguishing equipment while the area is made safe.\",
    \"Inspect the drain or sewer system, identify connected hazards, and isolate, cover, plug, seal, clean, purge, inert, or otherwise protect it as required by the permit and site procedure.\",
    \"Perform atmospheric testing for flammable vapor where sewer or drain vapor migration is possible, and continue monitoring if conditions can change.\",
    \"Do not resume until the authorized hot work permit is reviewed or reissued, controls are verified in the field, and the fire watch/fire protection requirements are in place.\"
  ],
  \"rationale\": \"Hot work over an uncontrolled sewer or drain is a sequence failure. NFPA 51B hot work controls require hazard assessment, permit authorization, protection of openings and combustible/flammable hazards, and fire watch/fire protection before work starts. OSHA 1926 Subpart J requires fire hazards to be controlled, openings protected from sparks and slag, and suitable fire extinguishing equipment available. Because the work is already in progress, it must fail and stop until the drain/sewer hazard is controlled and the permit conditions are verified.\",
  \"references\": [
    \"NFPA 51B - hot work permit and fire prevention precautions\",…
